failed.title: ISSUE_005: Rated as Critical (or highest) severity
failed.match: PASS if the software level of concern issue is rated as Critical, High, or the highest severity tier used in the memo. FAIL if it is rated as Moderate, Medium, Low, Minor, or a non-top-tier severity.

failed.title: ISSUE_010: Identifies missing PE risk warning in labeling
failed.match: PASS if the memo identifies that the draft IFU warnings section does not address the risk of pulmonary embolism (PE) from thrombus fragmentation and distal embolization during mechanical thrombectomy of large iliofemoral DVT. FAIL if this omission is not identified.

failed.title: ISSUE_012b: Identifies missing adverse event reporting procedures
failed.match: PASS if the memo identifies that the clinical study protocol does not address adverse event (AE) reporting procedures. FAIL if this omission is not identified.

failed.title: ISSUE_012c: Identifies missing tPA contraindication exclusion criteria
failed.match: PASS if the memo identifies that the clinical study exclusion criteria do not include patients with contraindications to tPA (such as recent stroke, intracranial neoplasm, active bleeding diathesis, or similar), despite the study protocol involving tPA administration. FAIL if this omission is not identified.

failed.title: ISSUE_013: Identifies incomplete contraindications list
failed.match: PASS if the memo identifies that the draft IFU lists only three contraindications (active internal bleeding, allergy to catheter materials, vessel diameter <6 mm) and that this is insufficient for a pharmacomechanical thrombectomy device, noting the need for additional contraindications such as contraindications to thrombolytic therapy, venous stenosis/occlusion proximal to treatment site, uncorrectable coagulopathy, or similar clinical contraindications. FAIL if the sparse contraindications list is not flagged as deficient.

failed.title: Memo correctly identifies product code GXD / 21 CFR 870.1250
failed.match: PASS if the memo references product code GXD and/or 21 CFR 870.1250 as the applicable classification regulation. FAIL if neither the product code nor the classification regulation is mentioned.

turns.text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

## Context management (automatic)

To keep your context small and your runs cheap, the harness automatically
elides the *raw text* of large `read`/`bash` outputs after they have been on
screen for a few turns, replacing each with a short `[context-saver: …]` stub
that names the file or command. The document was fully read when it was
current — nothing was hidden from your initial pass — only the bulk is dropped
from later turns. Two habits keep this lossless:

- As you read a docum

**CLEARFIELD MEDICAL DEVICES, INC.** *\\\[Logo\\\]* 8200 Excelsior Blvd, Suite 410 Minnetonka, MN 55343 Tel: (952) 555-0140 \| Email: regulatory@clearfieldmedical.com

May 19, 2025

Division of Cardiovascular Devices Office of Cardiovascular Devices Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) U.S. Food and Drug Administration 10903 New Hampshire Avenue Silver Spring, MD 20993

**Re: Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) Request --- VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System** **Pre-Submission Type: Traditional 510(k) Pre-Submission** **Proposed Product Code: GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\")** **Regulation Number: 21 CFR 870.1250**

Dear Members of the Division of Cardiovascular Devices:

**[I. Introduction and Purpose of the Pre-Submission]{.underline}**

Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. (\"Clearfield\"), a Delaware corporation headquartered in Minnetonka, Minnesota, respectfully submits this Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) package pursuant to the FDA guidance document entitled \"Requests for Feedback and Meetings for Medical Device Submissions: The Q-Submission Program\" (most recently revised June 2023). The purpose of this Pre-Submission is to request a meeting with the Division of Cardiovascular Devices to discuss the proposed regulatory pathway and submission strategy for the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System (the \"VascuClear 3000\" or the \"Device\").

Clearfield is a Class II medical device manufacturer founded in 2016 that specializes in the development of catheter-based vascular technologies. To date, Clearfield has received three prior 510(k) clearances from the Center for Devices and Radiological Health, all for Class II vascular access accessories, and has maintained full compliance with applicable Quality System Regulation (21 CFR Part 820) requirements throughout its history of commercial distribution. Clearfield intends to submit a traditional 510(k) premarket notification for the VascuClear 3000 and seeks the Division\'s feedback on the proposed regulatory strategy, predicate selection, indications for use, and testing plan prior to formal submission.

This Pre-Submission package has been prepared with the assistance of Clearfield\'s regulatory counsel, Hargrove, Templeton & Bliss LLP, 1750 K Street NW, Suite 900, Washington, DC 20006 (Katherine \"Kate\" Pressman, Partner; Daniel J. Yoo, Senior Associate), and Clearfield\'s regulatory and clinical strategy consultants, Ridgepoint Consulting Group, 3300 Gateway Centre Blvd, Suite 220, Morrisville, NC 27560 (Dr. Evelyn Marsh, RAC, Principal Consultant). Corporate and transactional matters for Clearfield are handled separately by Kendrick & Hale LLP and are not the subject of this submission; inquiries of a corporate nature may be directed to that firm.

**[II. Device Description Summary]{.underline}**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is an integrated catheter-based thrombectomy platform consisting of the following three principal components:

> 1\. **VascuClear Catheter.** A single-use, over-the-wire, 10 French (Fr) diameter, 65 cm working length catheter featuring a rotating helical impeller at the distal tip engineered for the mechanical maceration of thrombus. The catheter further incorporates a proprietary integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen designed to allow simultaneous local delivery of tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) directly at the thrombectomy site, thereby enabling pharmacomechanical thrombectomy through a single catheter platform.
>
> 2\. **VascuClear Console.** A reusable electromechanical drive unit that powers the helical impeller at rotational speeds up to 12,000 revolutions per minute (RPM) and houses an integrated aspiration pump capable of generating negative pressure up to −650 mmHg for continuous aspiration of macerated thrombus material.
>
> 3\. **Integrated Thrombolytic Infusion Lumen.** As an integral component of the VascuClear Catheter, the dedicated infusion lumen enables combined pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- the simultaneous mechanical removal of thrombus and localized delivery of thrombolytic agents --- through a single catheter insertion point, reducing procedure time and catheter exchanges.

The VascuClear 3000 is designed for the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the iliofemoral venous segment in adult patients presenting with acute proximal deep vein thrombosis (DVT) with symptom onset of fourteen (14) days or fewer. The key differentiating feature of the VascuClear 3000 is the integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen, which enables pharmacomechanical thrombectomy combining mechanical thrombus removal with simultaneous localized drug delivery, all within a single catheter platform. A complete description of the Device, including dimensional specifications, materials of construction, operating parameters, software architecture, and comparison with the proposed predicate device, is provided in the enclosed Device Description and Predicate Comparison document (Enclosure 1).

**[III. Proposed Predicate Device]{.underline}**

Clearfield has identified the following device as the sole proposed predicate for the VascuClear 3000:

> **Predicate Device:** ThrombEx 200 **Manufacturer:** Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc. (San Jose, CA) **510(k) Number:** K192847 **Clearance Date:** August 14, 2020 **Product Code:** GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\") **Regulation:** 21 CFR 870.1250 **Classification:** Class II

The ThrombEx 200 is an 8 French (Fr), 55 cm working length catheter-based thrombectomy device that utilizes a Bernoulli-effect aspiration mechanism for the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the peripheral vasculature. The ThrombEx 200 does not incorporate a rotating impeller and does not include any drug delivery capability. Its cleared indication for use is limited to the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the peripheral vasculature by mechanical aspiration.

Clearfield believes that the VascuClear 3000 is substantially equivalent to the ThrombEx 200 because both devices are catheter-based thromb

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[1. Introduction and Regulatory Context]{.underline}**

Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. (\"Clearfield\"), a Delaware corporation headquartered in Minnetonka, Minnesota, is a Class II medical device manufacturer founded in 2016 by a team of interventional radiologists with extensive experience in the management of venous thromboembolic disease. Since its founding, Clearfield has established a track record of successful regulatory submissions with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (\"FDA\"), having received 510(k) clearance for three Class II medical devices, all of which are accessories for vascular access procedures. These prior clearances demonstrate Clearfield\'s familiarity with the 510(k) regulatory framework and its commitment to developing safe and effective medical devices for the interventional vascular space.

Clearfield has developed the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System (the \"VascuClear 3000\" or \"Subject Device\"), a novel catheter-based mechanical thrombectomy system designed for percutaneous removal of thrombus and simultaneous local delivery of physician-specified thrombolytic agents in adult patients with acute deep vein thrombosis (\"DVT\") of the iliofemoral venous segment. The VascuClear 3000 represents Clearfield\'s entry into the interventional thrombectomy market and reflects significant investment in device engineering, preclinical bench testing, and clinical workflow analysis. The device integrates a rotating helical impeller for mechanical thrombus maceration, an aspiration system for thrombus removal, and an integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen for localized delivery of tissue plasminogen activator (\"tPA\") or other physician-specified thrombolytic agents directly at the treatment site during the thrombectomy procedure.

Clearfield intends to pursue a traditional 510(k) premarket notification for the VascuClear 3000 under product code GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\"), classified under 21 CFR 870.1250, Class II. This document, which constitutes Section 2 of the Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) package, provides a comprehensive device description for the VascuClear 3000, a summary of the proposed predicate device --- the ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter (K192847), manufactured by Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc. of San Jose, California --- and a detailed comparison demonstrating substantial equivalence between the Subject Device and the proposed predicate.

This Pre-Submission package has been prepared with the regulatory and clinical strategy support of Ridgepoint Consulting Group of Morrisville, North Carolina. Ridgepoint\'s principal consultant for this engagement, Dr. Evelyn Marsh, RAC, has provided guidance on predicate selection, the substantial equivalence argument, and the overall Pre-Submission strategy. Clearfield looks forward to engaging with FDA during the Pre-Submission meeting to obtain the Agency\'s feedback on the proposed regulatory pathway, predicate selection, and testing plan before preparing its formal 510(k) submission.

**[2. Device Description --- VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System]{.underline}**

**2.1 System Overview**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is a single-use (catheter component), over-the-wire catheter-based mechanical thrombectomy system designed for percutaneous removal of thrombus from the iliofemoral venous segment in adult patients with acute proximal deep vein thrombosis, defined as symptom onset within fourteen (14) days or fewer of the planned treatment. The system is intended to provide combined pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- that is, simultaneous mechanical thrombus removal and localized drug delivery --- through a single integrated catheter platform. This approach is designed to reduce treatment time and enable a comprehensive single-session intervention for appropriately selected patients.

The VascuClear 3000 system consists of three principal components:

> 1\. **VascuClear Catheter** --- a single-use, sterile, multi-lumen over-the-wire catheter incorporating a rotating helical impeller, an aspiration lumen, a guidewire lumen, and an integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen;
>
> 2\. **VascuClear Console** --- a reusable electromechanical drive unit that powers the catheter\'s rotating impeller, provides aspiration, and includes a touchscreen user interface for system control and monitoring; and
>
> 3\. **Integrated Thrombolytic Infusion Lumen** --- a dedicated lumen within the catheter for local delivery of physician-specified thrombolytic agents at the t

**[VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System]{.underline}**

**[Proposed Testing Plan]{.underline}**

**Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) Supporting Document**

Prepared by: **Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.** 8200 Excelsior Blvd, Suite 410 Minnetonka, MN 55343

In Collaboration with: **Ridgepoint Consulting Group** 3300 Gateway Centre Blvd, Suite 220 Morrisville, NC 27560

Principal Consultant: Dr. Evelyn Marsh, RAC Internal Contact: Linda Fessenden, VP of Regulatory Affairs, Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.

Document Date: May 2025 Document Version: Draft 2.1

**CONFIDENTIAL --- PRE-SUBMISSION DRAFT**

Regulatory Pathway: Traditional 510(k) Product Code: GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\") Regulation: 21 CFR 870.1250 Predicate Device: ThrombEx 200 (K192847) Predicate Manufacturer: Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc. Predicate Clearance Date: August 14, 2020

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[Section 1 --- Introduction and Scope]{.underline}**

The purpose of this document is to present the proposed performance testing plan for the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System in support of a future traditional 510(k) premarket notification submission to the Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) of the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA). This testing plan is submitted as a supporting document within Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.\'s Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) package and is intended to facilitate meaningful FDA feedback on the adequacy and completeness of proposed testing prior to initiation of formal studies.

The VascuClear 3000 is a single-use, over-the-wire catheter-based mechanical thrombectomy system consisting of three principal components:

1\. **VascuClear Catheter** --- A 10 French (Fr) single-use catheter with a 65 cm working length, featuring a rotating helical impeller at the distal tip for mechanical thrombus fragmentation and removal, and an integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen that permits simultaneous local delivery of tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) at the thrombectomy site.

2\. **VascuClear Console** --- A reusable electromechanical drive unit that provides variable-speed impeller rotation at speeds up to 12,000 revolutions per minute (RPM) and integrated aspiration pump capability generating negative pressure up to −650 mmHg for thrombus aspiration.

3\. **Proprietary Thrombolytic Infusion Lumen** --- Integrated into the catheter shaft, this dedicated lumen enables the treating physician to deliver physician-specified thrombolytic agents (e.g., tPA) locally at the site of thrombectomy during the mechanical thrombectomy procedure.

The intended use of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is the percutaneous removal of thrombus and simultaneous local delivery of physician-specified thrombolytic agents in adult patients with acute deep vein thrombosis (DVT) of the iliofemoral venous segment, with symptom onset within 14 days or fewer.

From a regulatory standpoint, Clearfield intends to submit the VascuClear 3000 via the traditional 510(k) pathway under product code GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\"), regulated under 21 CFR 870.1250. The identified predicate device is the ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter (K192847), manufactured by Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc. and cleared by CDRH on August 14, 2020.

The testing plan described herein is organized into seven categories, reflecting the full scope of performance and safety characterization required to demonstrate substantial equivalence to the predicate device:

> • Biocompatibility testing (Section 2)
>
> • Mechanical performance testing (Section 3)
>
> • Electrical safety testing (Section 4)
>
> • Software verification and validation (Section 5)
>
> • Sterilization validation (Section 6)
>
> • Simulated-use bench performance testing (Section 7)
>
> • Drug delivery characterization testing (Section 8)

This document should be read in conjunction with the companion documents included in the Pre-Submission package: the device description document (DOC*001), predicate comparison document (DOC*002), clinical synopsis (DOC*003), and draft Instructions for Use (DOC*005).

Testing will be conducted at Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories (biocompatibility and sterilization validation studies), Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc. (electrical safety and software verification and validation), and Clearfield Medical Devices\' internal R&D facility in Minnetonka, Minnesota (mechanical performance, bench performance, and drug delivery characterization). Each testing laboratory\'s qualifications and accreditations are further described in Appendix B.

**[Section 2 --- Biocompatibility Testing]{.underline}**

**[2.1 --- Overview and Rationale]{.underline}**

Biocompatibility testing for the VascuClear Catheter will be conducted in accordance with ISO 10993-1:2018, *Biological evaluation of medical devices --- Part 1: Evaluation and review within a risk management process*. The objective of the biocompatibility testing program is to evaluate the biological safety of the catheter materials that come into direct or indirect contact with the patient\'s blood and vascular tissue during clinical use.

The VascuClear Catheter is classified as a blood-contacting device with limited contact duration (less than 24 hours) per ISO 10993-1:2018, Table A.1. The catheter is inserted percutaneously for the thrombectomy procedure, which is typically completed within a single procedural session. Based on this classification, the biological evaluation endpoints have been selected from the matrix provided in Table A.1 of ISO 10993-1:2018 for devices with blood contact and limited exposure duration.

Given the limited contact duration classification, subchronic implantation testing per ISO 10993-6 is not applicable and has been explicitly excluded from the proposed testing plan. Similarly, subchronic systemic toxicity testing per ISO 10993-11 is not required for devices with limited contact duration and is excluded from the proposed program. Genotoxicity testi

**CONFIDENTIAL --- For FDA Pre-Submission Discussion Purposes Only**

**PROPOSED CLINICAL STUDY SYNOPSIS**

**VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System**

Prepared for Inclusion in Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) Package to the Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) U.S. Food and Drug Administration

**Sponsor:** Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. 8200 Excelsior Blvd, Suite 410 Minnetonka, MN 55343

**Document Prepared by:** Ridgepoint Consulting Group 3300 Gateway Centre Blvd, Suite 220 Morrisville, NC 27560 Principal Consultant: Evelyn Marsh, PhD, RAC

**Clinical Research Organization (Proposed):** Stonebridge Clinical Research Organization

**Regulatory Counsel:** Hargrove, Templeton & Bliss LLP 1750 K Street NW, Suite 900 Washington, DC 20006

**Document Date:** May 2025 **Version:** Draft 1.0 **Protocol Number:** CLR-VC3-001

*This document and its contents are the confidential and proprietary property of Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. This synopsis is prepared solely for Pre-Submission discussion with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and may not be disclosed, distributed, or reproduced without the prior written authorization of Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.*

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[1. Study Title and Protocol Identifiers]{.underline}**

**Proposed Study Title:** A Prospective, Single-Arm, Multicenter Evaluation of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System for Pharmacomechanical Thrombectomy in Adult Patients with Acute Iliofemoral Deep Vein Thrombosis

**Protocol Number:** CLR-VC3-001

**Sponsor:** Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc., 8200 Excelsior Blvd, Suite 410, Minnetonka, MN 55343

**Sponsor Contact:** Linda Fessenden, Vice President of Regulatory Affairs, Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.

**Clinical Research Organization:** Stonebridge Clinical Research Organization (proposed CRO responsible for study management, site monitoring, and data management)

**Proposed Study Start Date:** Q4 2025

**Estimated Enrollment Completion:** Q2 2026

**[2. Device Description Summary]{.underline}**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is a catheter-based platform designed to perform pharmacomechanical thrombectomy in the peripheral venous vasculature. The system is intended for the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the iliofemoral venous segment and consists of three principal components:

**(1) VascuClear Catheter.** The VascuClear Catheter is a single-use, 10 French (Fr) diameter, 65 cm working length, over-the-wire catheter designed for percutaneous insertion into the venous system. The catheter incorporates a rotating helical impeller at its distal tip that macerates and fragments thrombus upon contact. The catheter also features a proprietary integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen that enables the simultaneous local delivery of tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) directly at the thrombectomy treatment site. This integrated design allows for combined pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- mechanical thrombus fragmentation and aspiration occurring concurrently with localized thrombolytic drug delivery --- within a single catheter platform. The catheter is provided sterile and is intended for single use only.

**(2) VascuClear Console.** The VascuClear Console is a reusable electromechanical drive unit that powers the catheter\'s helical impeller at operator-selected speeds up to 12,000 revolutions per minute (RPM). The console also houses an integrated aspiration pump capable of generating negative pressure up to −650 mmHg, providing continuous aspiration of macerated thrombus material through the catheter lumen during the thrombectomy procedure. The console features a touchscreen user interface for real-time monitoring and adjustment of impeller speed and aspiration pressure. The console is designed for use in interventional radiology suites, catheterization laboratories, and operating rooms.

**(3) Thrombolytic Infusion Lumen.** The proprietary thrombolytic infusion lumen is integrated into the VascuClear Catheter shaft and provides a dedicated channel for the infusion of thrombolytic agents at controlled rates during the thrombectomy procedure. The infusion lumen connects to a standard intravenous infusion pump at its proximal hub and delivers the thrombolytic agent through multiple side-holes positioned along the distal treatment zone of the catheter, enabling uniform drug distribution across the target thrombus segment. This combined delivery architecture is a core feature of the VascuClear 3000 system and differentiates it from conventional mechanical-only thrombectomy catheters.

**Regulatory Classification.** The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is classified under product code GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\") and regulated under 21 CFR § 870.1250. The identified predicate device for the planned 510(k) submission is the ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System (K192847), manufactured by Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc., which received 510(k) clearance on August 14, 2020.

**[3. Study Rationale and Background]{.underline}**

Acute deep vein thrombosis (DVT) of the iliofemoral venous segment is a clinically significant condition that poses substantial morbidity to affected patients. Proximal DVT involving the iliac and common femoral veins carries an elevated risk of pulmonary embolism (PE) and is the primary risk factor for the subsequent development of post-thrombotic syndrome (PTS), a chronic condition characterized by pain, swelling, skin changes, and venous ulceration. Conventional treatment with systemic anticoagulation alone, while effective in preventing thrombus propagation, does not actively remove existing thrombus and may leave patients with significant residual venous obstruction and valve damage that contributes to the later development of PTS.

Pharmacomechanical thrombectomy has emerged as a treatment modality that combines the active mechanical removal of thrombus with the simultaneous local delivery of thrombolytic agents to the affected veno

… [clipped 27327 bytes]

CONFIDENTIAL

**CLEARFIELD MEDICAL DEVICES, INC.**

*\[Company Logo\]*

**[INSTRUCTIONS FOR USE]{.underline}**

**[VascuClear™ 3000 Thrombectomy System]{.underline}**

**DRAFT --- FOR REGULATORY REVIEW ONLY --- NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION**

Document Reference: IFU-VC3000-001, Rev. A

Date: May 2025

Product Code: GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\")

Regulation: 21 CFR 870.1250

Classification: Class II Medical Device

Prepared by: Regulatory Affairs Department Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. in collaboration with Ridgepoint Consulting Group

Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. 8200 Excelsior Blvd, Suite 410 Minnetonka, MN 55343

**CAUTION:** Federal law restricts this device to sale by or on the order of a physician.

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[1. Device Description]{.underline}**

The VascuClear™ 3000 Thrombectomy System is manufactured by Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. The VascuClear 3000 is a single-use, over-the-wire catheter-based mechanical thrombectomy system designed for the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the iliofemoral venous segment in patients with acute proximal deep vein thrombosis (DVT) with symptom onset of fourteen (14) days or less. The VascuClear 3000 is designed for combined pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- simultaneous mechanical thrombus removal and localized drug delivery.

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System comprises three principal components:

**1. VascuClear Catheter.** The VascuClear Catheter is a single-use, sterile, 10 French (Fr) diameter catheter with a 65 cm working length. The catheter incorporates a rotating helical impeller at the distal tip designed for mechanical maceration of thrombus within the target vessel. The catheter features an integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen that allows for simultaneous local delivery of thrombolytic agents --- such as tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) or other physician-specified thrombolytic agents --- directly at the thrombectomy site during mechanical thrombus removal. The catheter is compatible with a standard 0.035-inch guidewire and requires an introducer sheath of 11 Fr or greater. The catheter is supplied sterile in a peel-pouch package and is sterilized by ethylene oxide (EtO). The catheter is intended for single use only and must not be resterilized or reprocessed.

**2. VascuClear Console.** The VascuClear Console is a reusable electromechanical drive unit that powers the catheter\'s helical impeller at variable speeds up to a maximum of 12,000 revolutions per minute (RPM). The console includes an integrated aspiration pump capable of generating negative pressure up to −650 mmHg for thrombus aspiration. Console firmware controls impeller speed, aspiration pressure, and system status, and provides real-time operational feedback to the operator via an integrated LCD display. The display presents impeller speed, aspiration pressure, system alerts, and procedural status indicators. The console is designed for repeated clinical use and is intended to be cleaned and maintained per the manufacturer\'s instructions following each procedure.

**3. Proprietary Thrombolytic Infusion Lumen.** The thrombolytic infusion lumen is integrated into the VascuClear Catheter and allows simultaneous local delivery of tPA or other physician-specified thrombolytic agents at the thrombectomy site during mechanical thrombus removal. This feature enables pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- the concurrent application of mechanical thrombus maceration and aspiration with localized thrombolytic drug infusion --- within a single catheter platform.

The VascuClear Catheter may remain in situ for up to 72 hours for extended infusion therapy in cases where prolonged thrombolytic delivery is clinically indicated. When the treating physician determines that the initial thrombectomy session has not achieved satisfactory thrombus removal, the catheter may be left in position within the target vessel segment to allow continued thrombolytic infusion over an extended period, followed by a repeat thrombectomy session or catheter removal.

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is intended to provide an integrated approach to the management of acute iliofemoral DVT by combining mechanical thrombus removal and local thrombolytic drug delivery within a single, catheter-based platform.

**[2. Indications for Use]{.underline}**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is indicated for the percutaneous removal of thrombus and simultaneous local delivery of physician-specified thrombolytic agents in adult patients with acute deep vein thrombosis (symptom onset ≤14 days) of the iliofemoral venous segment.

The intended patient population is adult patients eighteen (18) years of age and older who present with acute proximal DVT involving the iliofemoral venous segment and who are candidates for catheter-directed thrombectomy. The indication is specific to the iliofemoral venous segment, which includes the common iliac vein, the external iliac vein, and the common femoral vein.

The device is intended for use by qualified physicians trained in interventional vascular procedures in a catheterization laboratory or equivalent hospital setting equipped with appropriate fluoroscopic imaging, hemodynamic monitoring, and resuscitation capabilities. The VascuClear 3000 is not intended for use in the arterial vasculature, the cerebral vasculature, or the pulmonary vasculature.

**[3. Contraindications]{.underline}**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is contraindicated in the following conditions:

1\. Active internal bleeding or a known condition predisposing the patient to active hemorrhage at a site other than the vascular access site.

2\. Known allergy or hypersensitivity to catheter component materials, including but not limited to polyurethane, stainless steel, and barium sulfate.

3\. Target vessel diameter less than 6 mm, as determined by pre-procedural imaging, due to increased risk of vessel injury from the 10 Fr catheter an

Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc. is the legal manufacturer and the sponsor of the 510(k) submission for the ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System.

**[Section 2: Indications for Use]{.underline}**

**Device Trade Name:** ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System

**510(k) Number:** K192847

**Product Code:** GXD

**Regulation Number:** 21 CFR 870.1250

**Indications for Use:**

The ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System is indicated for the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the peripheral vasculature.

The ThrombEx 200 is intended for use in hospital catheterization laboratories and interventional radiology suites by physicians trained in percutaneous interventional procedures. The device achieves thrombus removal through a purely mechanical aspiration mechanism. The ThrombEx 200 does not deliver any pharmaceutical agents, thrombolytic drugs, or therapeutic substances to the patient. The device is not indicated for use in conjunction with any drug delivery or drug infusion procedure.

The anatomical scope of the cleared indication encompasses the peripheral vasculature, including both the arterial and venous peripheral vascular systems. The indication is not limited to any specific vascular segment or anatomical region within the peripheral vasculature.

**Prescription Use.**

The ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System is restricted to prescription use in accordance with 21 CFR 801.109 and is intended for use only under the direct supervision of a physician.

**[Section 3: Device Description]{.underline}**

The ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System is a single-use, over-the-wire catheter-based mechanical thrombectomy system designed for the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the peripheral vasculature. The system consists of two primary components: the ThrombEx 200 Catheter and the ThrombEx Control Unit.

**3.1 ThrombEx 200 Catheter**

The ThrombEx 200 Catheter is a single-use, disposable, over-the-wire catheter designed for percutaneous introduction into the peripheral vasculature under fluoroscopic guidance. The catheter has the following specifications:

> • **Catheter Diameter:** 8 French (2.67 mm outer diameter)
>
> • **Working Length:** 55 cm
>
> • **Guidewire Compatibility:** 0.035-inch guidewire
>
> • **Lumen Configuration:** Single lumen (aspiration/working lumen)

The catheter shaft is constructed of a braided polyether block amide (Pebax) polymer reinforced with a stainless steel braid to provide a combination of flexibility, kink resistance, and pushability during catheter navigation through the vasculature. The inner surface of the catheter lumen is lined with a polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) liner to reduce friction and facilitate thrombus passage through the catheter lumen. The outer surface of the catheter shaft features a hydrophilic coating to reduce surface friction and enhance trackability during catheter insertion and navigation.

The distal end of the catheter includes a multi-side-hole aspiration region that consists of six radially distributed aspiration ports. These side holes, in combination with the end hole of the catheter, provide the aspiration interface through which thrombus is drawn into the catheter lumen during aspiration. A radiopaque marker band composed of barium sulfate-loaded polymer is positioned at the distal tip of the catheter to enable fluoroscopic visualization of the catheter tip position during the procedure.

The catheter has a single lumen design. There is no dedicated drug delivery lumen, no infusion port for thrombolytic agents, and no capability for simultaneous or sequential drug delivery. The catheter is designed exclusively as a conduit for mechanical thrombus aspiration.

**3.2 ThrombEx Control Unit**

The ThrombEx Control Unit is a reusable, non-sterile aspirati

tool_calls.cmd: which pandoc && pandoc --version | head -3 && echo "---SOFFICE---" && which soffice libreoffice 2>/dev/null; echo "---PYTHON---" && python3 -c "import docx; print('python-docx', docx.__version__)" 2>/dev/null || echo "no python-docx"

turns.result:
"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

# HARGROVE, TEMPLETON & BLISS LLP

**1750 K Street NW, Suite 900, Washington, DC 20006 \| Tel: (202) 555-0387**

## PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL --- ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATION / ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT

**Prepared in anticipation of the finalization of Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.'s Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) package to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. This memorandum contains attorney work product and is subject to the attorney-client privilege. Do not distribute outside the circulation list without the express consent of the authors.**

## ISSUE MEMORANDUM

  ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **TO:**                             Linda Fessenden, VP Regulatory Affairs, Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.

                                      Katherine "Kate" Pressman, Partner, Hargrove, Templeton & Bliss LLP

                                      Dr. Evelyn Marsh, RAC, Principal Consultant, Ridgepoint Consulting Group

  **FROM:**                           Daniel J. Yoo, Senior Associate, Hargrove, Templeton & Bliss LLP

  **DATE:**                           May 19, 2025

  **RE:**                             Prioritized Review of Draft Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) Package --- VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System

  **CLASSIFICATION:**                 Privileged & Confidential --- Attorney Work Product
  ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## I. Purpose and Scope of Review

At Kate Pressman's request (email of May 12, 2025), I have conducted a comprehensive review of the draft Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) package prepared for the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System ("VascuClear 3000" or "the Device") in advance of the targeted June 15, 2025 filing date. The package consists of the following five enclosures plus the internal strategy correspondence:

1.  **Pre-Submission Cover Letter** (presub-cover-letter.docx)
2.  **Device Description and Predicate Comparison** (device-description-predicate-comparison.docx)
3.  **Proposed Testing Plan** (proposed-testing-plan.docx)
4.  **Clinical Study Synopsis** (clinical-study-synopsis.docx)
5.  **Draft Instructions for Use** (draft-ifu-vasculear-3000.docx)
6.  **ThrombEx 200 (K192847) 510(k) Summary** (thrombex-200-510k-summary.docx)
7.  **Internal Email Chain --- Regulatory Pathway & Clinical Study Strategy** (internal-email-chain-reg-strategy.eml)

This memorandum identifies and prioritizes the regulatory, scientific, and strategic deficiencies I have identified. Issues are ranked in four tiers --- **Critical**, **High**, **Medium**, and **Lower/Strategic** --- based on (a) the potential to alter the regulatory pathway or trigger FDA rejection of the submission strategy, (b) the likelihood that FDA will raise the issue at the Pre-Submission meeting, and (c) the downstream impact on the 510(k) submission timeline and the Braddock Ventures Series D milestone (510(k) clearance by December 31, 2025).

The three **Critical** issues are threshold questions that, depending on FDA's response, could change the regulatory pathway, the reviewing office, the required evidentiary standard, or the feasibility of the December 31, 2025 clearance milestone. They should be resolved --- or at minimum explicitly posed to FDA --- before the package is filed.

## II. Executive Summary --- Priority Matrix

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  \#             Issue                                                                      Tier                  Documents Affected                                         Core Risk
  -------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1              Combination product classification under 21 CFR Part 3                     **Critical**          Cover Letter, Device Description, IFU, Clinical Synopsis   Could change pathway / reviewing office; cover letter does not ask FDA

  2              IDE requirement; "post-clearance" mischaracterization                      **Critical**          Clinical Synopsis, Cover Letter                            Study begins before clearance; device is investigational; no IDE question posed to FDA

  3              Substantial equivalence / predicate adequacy                               **Critical**          Device Description, Cover Letter, 510(k) Summary           Predicate has no drug delivery and no impeller; new questions of SE likely; no reference predicates

  4              Biocompatibility contact-duration contradiction (≤24 h vs. 72 h indwell)   **High**              Testing Plan, IFU, Device Description                      Wrong ISO 10993-1 category; excluded endpoints required

  5              Cover letter omits the two threshold questions counsel recommended         **High**              Cover Letter                                               FDA not asked the combination-product or IDE questions

  6              Impeller fatigue testing inadequate (500 revolutions ≈ 2.5 s)              **High**              Testing Plan, Device Description                           Fatigue test does not reflect clinical use

  7              EMC testing (IEC 60601-1-2) omitted                                        **High**              Testing Plan                                               Predicate had full EMC; FDA will require it

turns.name: read
turns.result:
**Where it appears:** Testing Plan (Section 5); Device Description (Section 2.3).

**The issue.** Two related problems: (a) the console firmware is classified as **"Minor"** level of concern, which is lower than the predicate's own software classification; and (b) the package relies on FDA's **2005** software guidance, which has been superseded.

**Analysis.**

-   **Level of concern / documentation level.** The console software directly controls a rotating helical impeller operating at up to 12,000 RPM inside a patient's blood vessel, plus an aspiration pump generating up to −650 mmHg. A software failure that causes uncontrolled impeller acceleration, failure to stop on command, or failure to detect an obstruction could plausibly result in serious injury (vessel perforation, dissection, hemolysis, embolization). The "Minor" rationale rests on the existence of independent hardware safety mechanisms (a mechanical over-speed governor and a hardware pressure-relief valve). Even assuming those mechanisms are present and validated, FDA may view a device that spins a blade at 12,000 RPM in a vein as warranting at least a **Moderate** (now "Enhanced") classification --- particularly because the predicate's own software was classified **Moderate** for a less active device (a saline-pressurization unit with no rotating element). The disparity will invite scrutiny.

-   **Outdated guidance.** FDA's 2005 guidance (*Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices*) used the "level of concern" (Minor/Moderate/Major) framework. FDA issued a successor guidance in 2023 (*Content of Premarket Submissions for Device Software Functions*) that replaced "level of concern" with a **"Documentation Level"** (Basic or Enhanced) and restructured the expected documentation. The package references the 2005 guidance throughout (Device Description Section 2.3; Testing Plan Sections 5.2, 5.3; References). The submission should be aligned to the current 2023 framework.

**Recommendation.** (a) Reassess the software documentation level under the 2023 guidance. Given the impeller control function and the serious-injury potential, an **Enhanced** documentation level is the more defensible position and would align the package with the predicate's treatment. (b) Update all references from the 2005 guidance to the 2023 guidance and restructure the proposed V&V activities (Section 5.3) accordingly --- at minimum, plan to include hazard analysis, architecture description, and anomaly tracking in the submission rather than withholding them. (c) Reference IEC 62304 for the software lifecycle (see Issue 19). (d) Verify and document the existence and validation of the hardware safety mechanisms on which the classification rationale depends.

### Issue 9 --- Clinical Study Design Weaknesses

**Tier: High**

**Where it appears:** Clinical Synopsis (Sections 5, 9, 10, 11, 12).

**The issue.** Beyond the IDE mischaracterization (Issue 2), the proposed study design has several features that FDA is likely to question.

**Analysis.**

1.  **Single-arm design with no comparator.** For a device with a new mechanism (rotating impeller) and a new feature (drug delivery) relative to the predicate, a single-arm study with an objective performance criterion (OPC) may be acceptable for 510(k) supportive evidence, but FDA may ask for a stronger design or a justified comparator. The OPC approach is only as credible as the performance goal and the historical data supporting it.

2.  **Performance goal (70%) and expected success rate (80%) are not justified against historical data.** The sample-size calculation assumes an 80% true success rate and a 70% performance goal, but the synopsis cites no historical control data, no prior study results, and no literature benchmark to support either number. The primary endpoint (≥50% thrombus removal at 24 hours) is a relatively low bar, and the 70% performance goal is asserted without derivation. FDA will ask where these numbers come from.

3.  **30-day follow-up cannot assess the clinically meaningful long-term outcome.** The principal long-term benefit of iliofemoral DVT intervention is reduction of post-thrombotic syndrome (PTS), which develops over months to years (the CaVenT and ATTRACT studies used 24-month and longer endpoints). A 30-day patency endpoint does not capture the outcome that matters most to patients and payors. FDA may request longer follow-up, particularly if the clinical data is to carry any weight in the SE determination.

4.  **No independent Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB).** Safety monitoring is assigned to the Sponsor and the CRO. For a study involving systemic thrombolytic infusion (with hemorrhagic risk) and a rotating intravascular device, an independent DSMB is typically expected. Its absence may be raised.

5.  **tPA dosing protocol (0.5--1.0 mg/hr alteplase) is specified but not justified.** The dose range should be tied to institutional standards and the drug labeling, and the rationale for the selected range should be stated.

6.  **The synopsis simultaneously claims the data will "support the 510(k) submission" and that the study is "post-clearance."** These are mutually exclusive: if the data supports the 510(k), the study must complete before submission (pre-clearance, investigational); if the study is post-clearance, the data cannot support the initial 510(k). This must be resolved (see Issue 2).

**Recommendation.** (a) Justify the performance goal and expected success rate with literature or historical data, or acknowledge to FDA that the OPC is proposed and request concurrence. (b) Consider extending follow-up to at least 6 months (and ideally toward a PTS assessment) or explain to FDA why 30-day data is adequate for a 510(k) supportive study. (c) Add an independent DSMB. (d) Resolve the pre-clearance/post-clearance contradiction. (e) Pose the study-design questions to FDA as already contemplated in cover-letter Questio

turns.text:
The deliverable is complete and validated. Here is a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverable: `output/presub-issue-memorandum.docx`

I reviewed all seven documents in the draft Pre-Submission package — the cover letter, device description/predicate comparison, proposed testing plan, clinical study synopsis, draft IFU, the ThrombEx 200 (K192847) 510(k) summary, and the privileged internal email chain — and prepared a prioritized issue memorandum written from the perspective of Daniel J. Yoo (Senior Associate), the reviewer tasked in the email thread. The memorandum is marked privileged/confidential attorney work product.

### Structure
- **Priority matrix** (executive summary table) ranking all 20 issues by tier
- **3 Critical issues** — threshold questions that could change the regulatory pathway
- **6 High-priority issues** — deficiencies FDA will likely raise
- **7 Medium-priority issues** — gaps to strengthen the submission
- **4 Lower/strategic issues** — timeline, human factors, standards, hemocompatibility
- **Sequenced action plan** keyed to the June 15, 2025 filing target

### The three Critical issues
1. **Combination product classification (21 CFR Part 3)** — the device has a purpose-built drug-delivery lumen and the indication claims thrombolytic delivery as a co-primary function, yet the cover letter does not ask FDA the combination-product question that counsel recommended.
2. **IDE requirement / "post-clearance" mischaracterization** — the study is labeled "post-clearance" but the package's own timelines show enrollment (Q4 2025) begins before the 510(k) is even submitted; the device is investigational under 21 CFR 812.3(m), and the IDE question is not posed to FDA.
3. **Substantial equivalence / predicate adequacy** — the sole predicate (ThrombEx 200) is purely mechanical with no drug delivery and no rotating impeller; the two differentiating features have no predicate basis, no reference predicates are identified, and the "no new questions of safety and effectiveness" assertion is unsupported.

### Notable High-priority findings
- Biocompatibility classified as "limited" (<24 h) contact while the IFU permits 72-hour indwell (prolonged contact) — excluded endpoints are actually required
- Impeller fatigue test of "500 revolutions" at 12,000 RPM equals ~2.5 seconds of operation
- EMC testing (IEC 60601-1-2) omitted though the predicate had a full EMC battery
- Software classified "Minor" (predicate was "Moderate") using the superseded 2005 guidance rather than the 2023 framework
- Clinical study: single-arm, 30-day follow-up (no PTS ass
